articles.title: China gives suspended death sentences to 2 ex-defense ministers
articles.summary: Both were the latest to be sentenced in President Xi Jinping's ongoing anti-corruption campaign, which started more than a decade ago.

articles.title: War and Energy Shortages Boost China’s Influence in Asia
articles.summary: The war in Iran has left China’s neighbors appealing for help, handing Beijing the kind of sway it has long sought.

articles.title: 5 Takeaways From the California Governor Debate
articles.summary: Candidates debated housing and insurance policy in the first half, then furiously attacked one another at the end.

articles.title: Judge Says F.B.I. Can Keep 2020 Election Records Seized From Georgia
articles.summary: The ruling is the latest in a saga driven by President Trump’s desire for redemption in the state, which he lost in 2020. The county is likely to appeal.

articles.title: These families help researchers find Alzheimer's treatments. Their network is at risk
articles.summary: Families with rare gene mutations that cause Alzheimer's in middle age are giving scientists a unique window on the disease, and a quick way to test potential treatments.

articles.title: Surging Gas Prices Shake Americans, and How Ted Turner Unleashed 24-Hour News
articles.summary: Plus, the conflicting messages on the state of the war.

articles.title: Florida Creates a More Conservative Course to Rival A.P. History
articles.summary: Here are ways Florida’s advanced high school history program, which students can use for college credit, differs from other curriculums.

articles.title: Susan Collins Says She Has Long Had a Benign Tremor
articles.summary: The Republican senator from Maine, running for re-election at age 73 in one of this year’s top Senate races, made the disclosure after mounting online scrutiny on the left.

articles.title: Iran reviews U.S. proposal. And, Rubio to meet Pope Leo after Trump's criticism
articles.summary: Iran is reviewing the Trump administration's latest proposal to end the war. And, Secretary of State Marco Rubio is meeting Pope Leo XIV amid President Trump's harsh criticism of him.

articles.title: As federal scientists faced turmoil, the Devils Hole pupfish reached a crisis point
articles.summary: The Devils hole pupfish lives in just one spot in Death Valley. Wildlife officials have managed this iconic fish for decades, and last spring, just as the Trump administration was laying off all kinds

articles.title: Psychiatrists say RFK Jr.'s take on SSRIs is an 'oversimplification' of the problem
articles.summary: The American Psychiatric Association says too few patients can access comprehensive mental health care in the United States. It welcomes new investments in improving access to evidence-based care.

articles.title: Gas prices keep rising, but do big oil companies plan to drill more? Not so far
articles.summary: The war in Iran has pushed global oil prices higher, which boosts oil company revenues. But major U.S. oil companies aren't signaling plans to increase production to bring down prices at the pump.

articles.title: Jeffrey Epstein’s Purported Suicide Note Is Released by Federal Judge
articles.summary: A federal judge released the note, which Jeffrey Epstein’s former cellmate said he found in a graphic novel. The New York Times has not authenticated that Mr. Epstein wrote it.

articles.title: Retracing Henry Knox’s Revolutionary War Trek Across the Wilderness
articles.summary: In 1775, George Washington sent Henry Knox, a Boston bookseller, to fetch 60 tons of artillery. We followed Knox’s route to see how the country has changed.

articles.title: Making a podcast helped one family talk about aging, dementia and death
articles.summary: This year's winner in NPR's College Podcast Challenge is a letter to a grandparent that grapples with health issues including dementia. It's the story of a family learning to talk about hard things.

articles.title: Dirty nickel: The cost of mining in Indonesia
articles.summary: Across six locations in Indonesia, NPR spoke with locals about how nickel mining is changing the land and daily life. It's brought jobs, but also concerns about environmental damage and public health.

articles.title: About 40 passengers previously left ship hit by Hantavirus at island of St. Helena
articles.summary: The dozens of passengers, including the wife of a Dutch man who died, left the cruise ship during a stop at the British territory, the Dutch foreign ministry said on Thursday.

articles.title: Affected by the Iran war, residents in Southeast Asian countries voice their concerns
articles.summary: Southeast Asian countries are voicing concern about President Trump and the war with Iran.

articles.title: Secretary of State Marco Rubio meets with Pope Leo after Trump's criticism
articles.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio meets with Pope Leo after critical comments by President Trump.

articles.title: Former U.S. ambassador to the Holy See on Rubio's visit to the Vatican
articles.summary: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with former U.S. Ambassador to the Holy See Miguel Diaz about Secretary of State Marco Rubio's visit to the Vatican.

articles.title: Ex-spy who sold U.S. secrets to Israel says he's sorry, and will run for parliament
articles.summary: Jonathan Pollard, a former U.S. intelligence analyst who served 30 years for spying for Israel, says he will run in upcoming Israeli elections.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
